quote_of_day.text: >>18144 Every religion is retarded. Praying in some church is retarded. Wasting minutes of your morning burning incense sticks while japping mantras is retarded. The whole point of religion is to do retarded things to feel closer to god by the rules some godmen told them centuries ago
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sacrilegious remarks moved over the board this morning, bringing with it a flurry of blasphemous comments about sacred animals and dietary laws.

Anon was in a heated debate, with one poster calling the reverence for certain animals 'retarded' and another defending the practice as a cultural norm.

A cold front of casteist remarks swept in during the late morning, bringing with it a batch of insults and jabs at regional identities.

The discussion was marked by a series of barbed comments about caste dynamics and regional heritage, with one poster claiming superiority based on genetic makeup.

A scattered drizzle of off-topic warnings and mocking remarks continued throughout the day, with one poster warning against replying to spammers and another mocking the appearance of a public figure, sparking a series of casteist remarks.

The atmosphere remained tense, with little sign of clearing by day's end.

articles.title: Anon Debates Sacred Animals and Dietary Laws
articles.body:
Ambedkar started the thread, saying having a sacred animal is 'the most retarded thing a culture could do' and that eating beef is 'based'.

He criticized the idea of a sacred cow, suggesting those who believe it are 'malnutritioned.' Anon responded, arguing that the concept of a sacred animal is tied to a spiritual layer that exists independently of moral or practical considerations.

Another anon noted that while cows are not considered gods, the idea of protecting them is rooted in their status as 'Aghniya,' and that some cows have mixed with other breeds.

A discussion on genetics and biology ensued, with anon pointing out that the Y chromosome is not inherently weaker than the X, but rather functions differently.

Another anon disagreed, stating that the Y chromosome is weaker because it cannot form a person on its own.

The conversation turned to dietary laws, with anon suggesting that choosing to follow certain dietary restrictions is a matter of faith rather than morality.

Anon also mentioned researching the 'Indus Valley Diet,' which includes meat and seafood, and aims to recreate it with modifications. Narthie criticized users who 'shitter' threads, saying that a few users ruined a good discussion.

Anon expressed frustration that threads often devolve due to 'dedicated thread shitters.' The thread also touched on the idea that symbols of faith can be bastardized, and that having power to defend one's beliefs is important.

Anon noted that even if one does not agree with certain beliefs, they can still start their own branch of a faith, citing the example of adding another branch to the 'dharmic tree.' Haggu and others engaged in a side discussion on genetics and racial purity, with Haggu arguing that certain populations have undergone genetic bottlenecks, but that 'muttification' is not necessarily a bad thing.

The conversation remained contentious and complex, reflecting a range of perspectives on faith, biology, and culture.

articles.title: Caste Dynamics and Regional Identity
articles.body:
Anon sparked a discussion on caste and regional identity, claiming that South Indian Brahmins create "beautiful temple, mathematics art" and suggesting that Tamil Dalits have been oppressed since 5,000 BCE.

Anon responded by questioning the idea that certain castes are inherently superior, citing examples of castes in different regions. Another anon countered that some castes, such as Konkan Brahmins, are considered pure and deserving of respect.

One respondent argued that genetic studies show all Indians are on a genetic cline, with Brahmins and Dalits not being distinct groups.

Anon disagreed, stating that Dwija status is a social construct that doesn't necessarily correlate with wealth or assets. The conversation turned to the topic of regionalism, with Anon stating that Upper Castes (UCs) in different regions have distinct characteristics.

Another anon pointed out that Dalits and other lower castes are often pawns in larger social and political games.

A respondent noted that Brahmins have historically been a middle-class caste, excelling in academia and government, but not necessarily in wealth or land ownership.

Anon observed that some Lower Castes (LCs) try to claim UC status or associate with UCs to gain social standing.

The discussion also touched on the idea that caste purity and Varna systems are often opposed by landowning castes, and that regional identities are complex and multifaceted.

Anon suggested that some castes, such as Pasmanda Chamars, can be considered UCs despite being from lower castes.

articles.title: Erotic Asphyxiation Discussed on Imageboard
articles.body:
Anon described a 24-year-old Onlyfans and PornHub erotic gay model who died in Italy during the COVID-19 pandemic. The model was livestreaming with a radio news station playing in his room when his location and corpse were discovered 24 hours later.

Ambedkar shared several cases of individuals who died from erotic asphyxiation. A 23-year-old woman died after her partner waited too long to release her during a strangulation session, causing a heart attack.

Another case involved an 18-year-old man who streamed his own death on an erotic platform, using a bed sheet as a noose.

Ambedkar also discussed the debate over whether some individuals who engage in erotic asphyxiation are seeking death or simply miscalculate the risks. He noted that people can become addicted to the powerful orgasms associated with this fetish.

Fuwawa wondered how people discover their affinity for autoerotic asphyxiation, suggesting that experimentation and curiosity play a role. Gura shared a personal experience, stating that while they have watched gore videos since age 13, hanging videos make them uncomfortable.

Soythie posted a lengthy string of 'a's and 'h's, while another poster described a case of a young man who died after being strangled by two women during a sex game.

Anon joked that the women should have masturbated the man's dead body as a "last goodbye". The discussion also touched on the topic of masochism, with Soythie suggesting that it may be induced by past traumas.

Dyaush questioned the appeal of hanging as a fetish, stating that no sane person would engage in such a risky behavior.

articles.title: User warns against replying to spammer
articles.body: Fuwawa posted a warning not to reply to a spammer who is active on the board. The spammer is allegedly from 4chan's /r9k/ and doesn't understand the local context. Fuwawa suggested that ignoring the spammer would lead to the thread being automatically deleted, rather than requiring manual deletion. Other users, including Nepajeet and Gura, discussed and supported this approach, with some noting that the spammer's efforts seemed futile.

articles.title: Anon mocks Chirag Paswan's appearance, sparks casteist remarks
articles.body:
Anon joked about Chirag Paswan's appearance, calling his father "subhumanoid" and mother having "better genes." The poster sarcastically suggested Paswan's genes were "upgraded" through his mother's supposed upper-caste (UC) connections.

Anon respondents made casteist remarks, with one suggesting Dalits should be killed or exiled to a separate nation.

Another anon stated that upper castes should separate from India, with Dalits forming their own nation, "Bheemrashtra." The discussion was marked by several racist and casteist comments, including calls for violence against Dalits and claims that they are "subhuman" or "seweroids."
